subject: Bolas's trap sprung over a war-torn city — a vast circular sigil of orange-gold light blazes in the sky above ruined towers and crumbling ramparts
description: A massive circular rune of fiery orange and white light flares overhead like a searing sigil-eye, its concentric inner glyphs flaring against a deep blue night sky. Below, jagged silhouettes of ruined fortress walls, broken towers, and collapsing spires rise in dark indigo and black, with smoke and embers drifting between them. The horizon glows with the fire of distant battle. The composition is wide and architectural, no figures present — the viewer stands at street level looking up into the sealing trap. The mood is one of inescapable doom, the blue-and-orange palette pure War of the Spark Bolas-trap iconography.
foreground: ruined city walls and broken battlements in deep indigo silhouette, rubble and shattered stone
background: enormous glowing circular rune of orange-gold light filling the night sky, fiery clouds and embers, distant burning horizon
emotion: no figures — atmospheric dread, inescapability, the moment a trap snaps shut
